title: Sideroxylon dongnaiense
taxonomy: Sideroxylon dongnaiense is classified within the family Sapotaceae and the order Ebenales. It belongs to the genus Sideroxylon, which is a group of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species Sideroxylon dongnaiense is further classified as a species within the genus Sideroxylon, and its classification is based on its unique characteristics and genetic features. Sideroxylon dongnaiense is a part of the family Sapotaceae, which includes a wide range of plants commonly known as sapotes. The classification of Sideroxylon dongnaiense is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of taxonomy.
uses: Sideroxylon dongnaiense, also known as the Dong Nai ironwood, has been found to possess medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in Vietnamese medicine to treat various health issues. Its bark and roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
